turnaround_reason: Helen of Troy was hit by retailer destocking, OXO/Hydro Flask brand softness, tariff exposure, and a big goodwill write-down driving GAAP losses. The stock has already ripped +39% in 30 days post-earnings beat, suggesting the turnaround leg is underway but late-entry risk is high.
bullish_points: Two consecutive EPS beats with Q4 surprise +17.8%
Trading 28% above the 200-day MA with strong 30-day momentum (+39%)
$155M positive free cash flow at $600M market cap — FCF yield 26%
Forward P/E 6.3x reflects deep value if revenue stabilizes
Strong brand portfolio (OXO, Hydro Flask, Osprey, Vicks) with category leadership
Price-to-book 0.75x — trading below tangible asset value
bearish_points: Revenue declining -3.3% YoY; profit margin -50% TTM (impairment-driven)
Zero insider open-market buying despite the prolonged drawdown
Already up ~85% off the 52-week low — momentum chase risk
Tariff/China supply exposure remains structural risk
Implied vol of 106% suggests options market expects continued volatility — premium expensive
catalyst: Q1 FY27 earnings (July 9) — the next test of the recovery thesis
suggested_trade: Spot only, sized small — options too expensive given IV. Better setup is to wait for pullback to $20-22 area
key_risk: Q1 print misses or guides down on tariff hit, triggering 25%+ retrace given the recent run-up
would_skip_if: Earnings disappoint; tariff news worsens; price gets within 10% of 52w high before reset
